abstract: A map of community-led AI initiatives that makes it easier to discover, connect, and build across Southern Africa.
rationale: The project exists to turn scattered local signals into one coherent field of trust, collaboration, and momentum.

abstract: A conceptual studio for turning failed ideas into sharp, public-facing prototypes that reveal what learning actually feels like.
rationale: Rethinking failure as a signal makes the work more honest, more human, and more useful for the next iteration.

abstract: A narrative-first digital archive that pairs long-form reflection with interactive storytelling and project context.
rationale: The story behind a build matters as much as the build itself, especially when the work lives in motion.

abstract: A late-night prototype playground where concepts are tested in public and shaped by the feedback of the people who actually use them.
rationale: The best ideas often arrive when the room is quiet and the pressure is low enough to let them breathe.

abstract: A modular storytelling system that threads together talks, notes, and experiments into one coherent public record.
rationale: A project should keep its memory alive even after the spotlight moves on.

abstract: An exploratory framework that helps communities locate the next useful opportunity in the middle of uncertainty and noise.
rationale: Direction is often built by noticing patterns before the world names them.
